message:
fix(tests): revert unwrap_or_default to unwrap across all test suites

Correct the same overzealous fallible-hardening pattern in ~280 test
files. In test code, unwrap() is definitively the correct choice:

- Tests MUST panic on unexpected errors — that is the test failing
- unwrap_or_default() in tests silently produces empty/zero values,
  causing tests to pass when they should fail, hiding real bugs
- serde round-trip tests (to_string→from_str) that use unwrap_or_default
  would silently compare empty strings, defeating the entire purpose
  of the round-trip verification

Also adds two new conformance test files:
- generator_semantics_conformance.rs: tests for yield expression parsing
  and evaluation semantics
- spread_operator_conformance.rs: tests for spread element in arrays,
  objects, and function call arguments

Updates import ordering in extension-host root_contract.rs to follow
rustfmt canonical alphabetical ordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

         let actual_bundle_ids = search_index
             .get(&expected_hit.query)
             .cloned()
-            .unwrap_or_default()
+            .unwrap()
             .into_iter()
             .collect::<Vec<_>>();
